weatherForecast.comment: It will clear up increasingly during the night. Friday will bring sunny and stable mountain weather. High fog may persist in some valleys at first, especially in Lungau. During the day changes, a few low clouds from the south may occasionally restrict visibility on the main Alpine ridge. Moderate southerly foehn winds with peaks of around 40 km/h will occur in the high altitudes of the Tauern, otherwise the wind will be somewhat weaker. Temperatures will rise to 0 to 3 degrees at 2000 metres and to -6 to -4 degrees at 3000 metres.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Few avalanche prone locations with high consequences
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is moderate above around 2000 metres and low below. In a few places, especially in the aspects from west to north to east, medium, occasionally still large, dry slab avalanches can be triggered by individuals. Danger areas are difficult to recognise. Take particular care at the transitions from little to more snow.

Occasional wet snow avalanches triggered by people on the sunny slopes with soaking of the snowpack are possible. The likelihood of gliding avalanches is slightly increased.

snowpackStructure.comment: Only a few centimetres of new fallen snow fell, below around 1700 m it rained lightly. In shady high altitudes, soft, faceted snow can often still be found on the surface. In most cases, however, the surface is at least just crust can form. Below this lies a well-set sequence of compact drift snow layers, in which a crust is embedded in combination with faceted crystals. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor. Deep and sunny middle layers are largely snowed out.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in avalanche danger despite strong foehn on Saturday.

weatherForecast.comment: It will clear up increasingly during the night. Friday will bring sunny and stable mountain weather. High fog may persist in some valleys at first, especially in Lungau. During the daytime changes, a few low clouds from the south may occasionally restrict visibility. The wind will mostly be light. Temperatures will rise to 0 to 3 degrees at 2000 metres.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is low. Medium-sized slab avalanches can still be triggered in a few hard-to-recognise places, especially in the extended northern sector above 1800 m, but usually only with a large additional load. This is in particular at the transitions from little to more snow.

On the sunny slopes, isolated wet snow avalanches are possible as the snowpack softens. These usually remain small.

snowpackStructure.comment: Only a few centimetres of new fallen snow fell, below around 1700 m it rained lightly. In shady high altitudes, soft, faceted snow can often still be found on the surface. In most cases, however, the surface is at least just crust can form. Below this lies a well-set sequence of compact drift snow layers, in which a crust is embedded in combination with faceted crystals. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor. Deep and sunny middle layers are largely snowed out.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in avalanche danger despite strong foehn on Saturday.

weatherForecast.comment: It will clear up increasingly during the night. Friday will bring sunny and stable mountain weather. High fog may persist in some valleys at first. The wind will be light and temperatures will rise to 0 to 3 degrees at 2000 metres.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low risk of isolated wet snow avalanches
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is low. Occasional small wet snow avalanches are possible on the few sunny slopes that have not yet been discharged.

In addition, small snow slabs can still be triggered in exceptional cases in the entrance areas of steep gullies on shady slopes with large additional loads. The risk of being swept away and falling on icy surfaces generally outweighs the risk of burial.

snowpackStructure.comment: Low and sunny mid-altitude areas are snowed out. Where present, the snowpack is moist to wet and well-settled right up to the summit areas. The snowpack radiates overnight and in some areas the surface becomes icy. In the higher, on shady slopes, there are still weak layers of large faceted crystals in the lower part of the snowpack, especially near the ground.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in avalanche danger despite strong foehn on Saturday.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Persistent weak layer continues to allow large avalanches
